_comment: Seccomp profile for the NETWORK-LESS render worker (render_worker.py, issue #2).
Deny-by-default (SCMP_ACT_ERRNO): only the listed syscalls are allowed, and all
networking syscalls (socket/connect/bind/sendto/...) are deliberately omitted, so
scene code cannot open a socket even though it never should reach one anyway.
This is for the render service ONLY (docker-compose.yml applies it there). The web
container needs sockets to serve HTTP and call the Anthropic API, so it keeps
Curated and VERIFIED on CPython 3.12 + Pillow + multiprocessing fork on x86_64
(glibc): a full render and the multiprocessing SemLock path both succeed, and
socket() is denied. It is kernel/glibc/arch-sensitive — if a render fails on your
platform, strace the worker for the EPERM syscall and add it, or fall back to
'--network none' + Docker's default seccomp (drop the seccomp line in compose),
which still fully isolates the render path from the network. See DEPLOY.md.
